WTI.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $91.66 (-4.4%) vs history prev_close $88.13 (-0.6%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

Gold.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $4346.80 (+4.6%) vs history prev_close $4404.10 (+3.2%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: Markets closed Tuesday with modest broad-market losses (SPY -0.54%, QQQ -0.93%) while energy surged on the Hormuz crisis now entering week 3. The dominant macro drivers remain unchanged: the Fed's rate pause at 3.50%-3.75% keeps the 10-year yield structurally elevated at 4.39%, compressing tech multiples, while the Strait of Hormuz disruption provides a geopolitical floor under oil prices. Per Yahoo Finance, futures jumped after the New York Times reported the US offered Iran a peace deal — if confirmed, this could be the most significant overnight development this week, with potential for a sharp VIX compression and tech relief bounce at today's open. PLTR surged on Golden Dome defense software contract news per Reuters, while GOOG dropped 3.31% on above-average volume — a meaningful deterioration worth watching.
commodities_note: Gold data shows a conflict: the fast-info source implies a +4.6% daily move to $4,545, while the historical close implies +3.2% from $4,404. Using the fast-info headline figure of $4,545 — either way, gold is at or near record highs, signalling persistent safe-haven demand. No entry opportunity exists at this extension; GLD is the vehicle if a pullback materialises. WTI also has a data anomaly: fast-info implies -4.4% to $87.63, while the prior historical close of $88.13 implies only -0.6%. The 5-day range in news digests shows WTI oscillating in the $91-98 range, suggesting the -4.4% daily figure may reflect a data artefact rather than a true collapse — use $87-88 as the approximate level with caution. The peace-deal headline, if confirmed, could push WTI sharply lower at the open, unwinding the Hormuz premium. DXY softened slightly to 99.16 (-0.23%), consistent with the risk-on tilt from the Iran peace-deal news.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. DBS is 1.6% below a declining 50-day average of SGD 57.54 with RSI at 49.5 — technically neutral-bearish and entering its 15th consecutive cautious session. The structural headwind is NIM compression: with the Fed holding rates at 3.50%-3.75% and the 10-year yield at 4.39% (moving away from the 4.20% reset threshold), DBS's net interest margin recovery thesis has no near-term catalyst. Earnings are 36 days out (April 30). Revenue growth is running at -3.3% and consensus projects an -8.8% earnings decline — not a thesis-breaking collapse, but meaningful deterioration that argues against entry. Valuation is in line with the Singapore bank cluster median at 13.7x forward PE, so there's no discount to compensate for the headwinds. The R:R is unfavourable at 0.5:1 — upside to SGD 58.73 resistance is 3.7% versus 7.2% downside to the SGD 52.53 support invalidation. If the Iran peace deal materialises and oil eases, Singapore banks could catch a relief bid, but that's speculative. The 5.4% dividend yield is a source of patience, not a reason to enter. Bottom line: DBS is fairly priced for what it is, but the earnings trend is heading the wrong way. Wait for US10Y to break below 4.20% before reconsidering.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to WATCH — OCBC has crossed above its rising 50-day average of SGD 21.04 for the first time in the current cycle, with RSI at 51.9 in neutral-bullish territory. Invalidation is at SGD 21.04 (the 50-day SMA, 0.6% below current price) — a tight stop, and normal intraday volatility could trigger it before the thesis is tested. The tight invalidation flag applies here: structural support at the 200-day average of SGD 18.23 (13.8% below) is the more meaningful thesis test if the SMA break fails. The R:R using the nearest resistance at SGD 21.81 is 5.2:1, which is technically attractive, but rr_quality is observed and the stop is very tight. The PEG ratio of 4.49 is elevated, reflecting modest earnings growth of only 3.7% projected — OCBC is not cheap on a growth-adjusted basis. However, OCBC is the only Singapore bank with demonstrated NIM resilience, trading at a 13.1% discount to the cluster median PE of 13.7x and offering a 3.97% yield. The primary watch condition: price holding above SGD 21.04 with volume expansion would confirm the breakout. Volume is currently normal (not above average), so no volume confirmation yet. Bottom line: OCBC is modestly cheap relative to peers with the cleanest technical setup in the Singapore cluster. Worth watching, but the stop is too tight to enter aggressively today — volume confirmation is the trigger.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. UOB is 2.8% below its 50-day average of SGD 37.39 with RSI at 43.8 — neutral-bearish with low volume, signalling no conviction either way. The 50-day SMA is rising, which is a mild positive, but the stock has been unable to reclaim it. Analyst consensus is HOLD with a projected -6.7% earnings decline — consistent with NIM compression across Singapore banks. At 10x forward PE (27% discount to the cluster median of 13.7x), UOB is the cheapest Singapore bank, but the PEG of 2.34 reflects the modest growth backdrop. The R:R is 3.7:1 to the SGD 39.49 resistance with invalidation at the 200-day average of SGD 35.51 (2.3% below). ACCUMULATE gate check: caution_source is base_scorer and R:R is 3.7:1 with observed quality, BUT revenue growth is -1.8% and earnings are projected to decline 6.7% — this is not a thesis break but a cyclical compression. The structural NIM compression narrative has run for 15 sessions with no reset signal. ACCUMULATE is not warranted here because the growth trajectory is deteriorating and the macro conditions driving the CAUTION (US10Y above 4.20%) remain unresolved. The 200-day SMA at SGD 35.51 is the next structural support, 2.3% below. Bottom line: UOB is cheap but getting cheaper for a reason — avoid until US10Y resets below 4.20% or NIM trends stabilise.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Singapore Airlines is 1.9% below its 50-day average of SGD 6.64, RSI at 41.7 — technically weak with no entry catalyst. The Hormuz crisis is the central risk: secondary logistics costs from tanker diversions and air cargo reroutes compound margin pressure even beyond direct fuel exposure. The critical concern is the analyst consensus: earnings are projected to decline 64.3% — this MUST be weighed prominently. A >50% expected earnings decline is incompatible with a constructive stance unless already priced in or a concrete recovery catalyst exists within 2 quarters. At 17.1x forward PE (24.8% premium to the 13.7x cluster median), SIA is actually expensive relative to peers despite this earnings trajectory — the valuation is not compensating for the risk. The one fundamental positive is the 8.4% FCF yield, which provides some balance sheet resilience. The R:R is unfavourable at 0.8:1 with structural support at SGD 5.57 (14.4% below). The 5.38% dividend yield is attractive but yields can be cut if earnings drop as projected. Bottom line: SIA is expensive relative to peers with a 64% earnings decline expected — avoid until the Hormuz situation resolves or the stock reprices meaningfully lower to compensate.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Singtel is 1.9% above its rising 50-day average of SGD 4.83, RSI at 47 — the rally stalled after a strong 36-session run above the 50-day SMA and the 5-day reading is -4.84%, suggesting a pullback is underway. Invalidation is at SGD 4.83 (the 50-day SMA, 1.8% below) — tight enough that the tight invalidation disclosure applies. Structural support at the 200-day average of SGD 4.36 (11.4% below) is the more meaningful thesis test if the SMA breaks. The R:R to nearest resistance at SGD 5.15 is 2.6:1 with observed quality. The valuation picture is mixed: at 24.3x forward PE, Singtel trades at a 77.4% premium to the Singapore cluster median of 13.7x — elevated for a telecom with only 0.9% revenue growth. The PEG of 3.8 confirms it is expensive for the growth it delivers. Earnings growth data is unavailable. The bull case rests on Singtel's regional digital infrastructure pivot (Optus, data centres) rather than near-term earnings momentum. ACCUMULATE gate check: caution_source is base_scorer and R:R is 2.6:1, but revenue growth of 0.9% versus a 24.3x forward PE fails the revenue growth vs PE fundamental check — growth is far less than half the multiple. Watch condition: holding above SGD 4.83 with volume recovery. Bottom line: Singtel's technical setup is reasonable but the valuation is hard to justify at current growth rates — wait for a pullback to SGD 4.83-4.90 before considering entry.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVDA is 4.9% below a declining 50-day average of $184.21, RSI at 41.5 — neutral-bearish with no volume confirmation. Invalidation for any long thesis is at $169.81 (3.1% below), with structural support at $164.30 (6.2% below). The nearest resistance is $184.47. ACCUMULATE gate check: all mechanical gates pass (caution_source=base_scorer, R:R=1.7:1). However, R:R of 1.7:1 is below the 2.0:1 threshold required for ACCUMULATE — signal stays CAUTION. The fundamental picture remains exceptional: NVDA's CEO projects the era of AI-run companies is already here, and the Blackwell Ultra ramp per the Q4 FY2026 earnings call guides $78B for Q1 FY2027. That is a bullish call from a seller and should be treated accordingly, but the fundamentals are independently strong — 73.2% revenue growth, 95.6% earnings growth consensus, 0.71 PEG at only 15.8x forward PE (a 17% discount to the semis cluster median). The FCF yield of 1.37% is low, meaning the thesis is priced on future growth delivery. Export pressure continues per GuruFocus, with lawmakers pushing tighter AI chip controls — a real risk to the China revenue stream. The Iran peace-deal news could trigger a relief rally in tech today. Bottom line: NVDA is genuinely cheap relative to its growth rate — the best fundamental case on the watchlist. But the trend is still down. Wait for the 50-day average to flatten and price to reclaim $184 with volume before entering.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — AMD has recovered 4.6% over the past 5 sessions, RSI has rebuilt to 51.2 (neutral-bullish), and the stock is closing the gap to its 50-day average of $214.45 (currently 4.2% below). This is not yet a WATCH signal because price has not reclaimed the 50-day SMA, but the deterioration that justified CAUTION has reversed. The Meta GPU supply deal — confirmed via AMD IR press release, Meta newsroom, and CNBC — is the fundamental anchor: up to 6GW of AMD Instinct GPUs with performance-based warrants, first 1GW shipments H2 2026, validating AMD as a credible NVDA alternative at hyperscale. Deutsche Bank analysts, per Investopedia, note AMD looks like a bargain at current levels alongside NVDA. At 19.1x forward PE with a 0.6 PEG and 34.1% revenue growth, AMD is priced at its cluster median with a growth rate that materially exceeds its multiple — the most attractive valuation setup in the semis cluster after NVDA. The R:R to the nearest resistance at $218.84 is 1.2:1 with invalidation at the 200-day average of $193.84 — not compelling enough for ACCUMULATE. Earnings are 42 days out. The 217% projected earnings growth consensus is extraordinary, but FCF yield at 1.37% means execution risk remains. Bottom line: AMD is cheap for its growth rate and recovering technically. Upgrades to WATCH on a reclaim of $214 with volume; the Meta deal makes this the highest-conviction waiting name on the watchlist.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 44x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Intel is 5.4% below a rising 50-day average of $46.57, RSI at 46 — neutral-bearish with low volume. The entry block remains in force: the pipeline flags a CAUTION on the 44.5x forward PE, which requires explicit earnings growth justification. The problem is that Intel's earnings growth consensus is unavailable, revenue is declining -4.1%, and FCF yield is -2.05% (negative cash flow — flag this prominently). At 133% premium to the semis cluster median PE of 19.1x, Intel is the most expensive semiconductor name relative to peers despite no visible earnings growth catalyst. The CHIPS Act 18A foundry roadmap is real progress, but earnings on April 24 (30 days away) represent a binary event that could either validate or further compress the thesis. The R:R to the nearest resistance at $46.97 is 1.9:1 with invalidation at $42.58 (3.4% below) — structural support at $34.82 (21% below) represents significant downside if the foundry thesis fails. The next support between current price and the structural floor at $34.82 is at $42.58. Bottom line: Intel is expensive relative to peers with negative FCF — the 18A thesis is real but the valuation requires proof of earnings recovery. Avoid until the April 24 earnings clarify the trajectory; the risk/reward does not justify entry ahead of a binary print.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — ASML has crossed back above its rising 50-day average of $1,392.48 for the first time since turning cautious, RSI at 52.6 neutral-bullish, with a confirmed $7.9B SK Hynix EUV investment (reported by GuruFocus, corroborated by Investor's Business Daily) providing a direct demand catalyst. The SK Hynix deal — securing advanced chipmaking equipment through 2027 as AI-driven memory demand intensifies — is an independent proof point for ASML's EUV order book strength beyond the Intel High-NA ramp. However, the invalidation is extremely tight: at the 50-day SMA of $1,392.48, just 0.5% below. This triggers the minimum invalidation distance disclosure: tight stop — normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use the structural support at $1,030.63 (26.4% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. The R:R to the nearest resistance at $1,407.54 is only 1.2:1 — this is a WATCH signal, not actionable yet. The forward PE of 32.1x at a PEG of 2.2 is elevated for 4.9% revenue growth, though the 2030 revenue trajectory of $71B (per company guidance) implies a much longer growth runway. Volume is normal — no volume confirmation of the breakout. Earnings are 21 days out on April 15 — a binary event that argues for patience. Bottom line: ASML is fairly priced for its monopoly position, not cheap. The SMA reclaim is encouraging but the tight stop and upcoming earnings make this a watch-and-confirm setup rather than an entry today.

MU.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — MU has dropped 14.3% over 5 sessions following its blowout Q2 FY2026 earnings (record revenue, HBM demand confirmed), a classic 'buy the rumour, sell the news' pattern that erased the WATCH signal conditions. RSI has fallen to 45.6, and MU is now 1.9% below its 50-day average of $403.16. The 30% dividend increase announced per Barchart is a positive signal from management on cash generation, but the valuation picture deserves careful scrutiny: forward PE of 4.0x with a PEG of 0.26 and 756% projected earnings growth appear extraordinary — these figures are anomalous enough to warrant caution. When forward PE diverges from trailing PE (4.0x vs 19.1x) by this magnitude, it signals expected earnings inflection, not normalised multiples. The FCF yield of 0.65% is low, and the 15.0% reported dividend yield appears anomalous — verify before citing as a primary investment thesis. The R:R to resistance at $437.83 is 1.3:1 with invalidation at $363.90 (8% below) — not compelling enough for ACCUMULATE. ACCUMULATE gate check: R:R of 1.3:1 falls below the 2.0:1 threshold. Structural support at $357.67 (9.6% below) represents meaningful downside. The post-earnings selloff despite strong results suggests the market is pricing in peak cycle concerns — MU earnings could be the high-water mark before memory pricing softens. Bottom line: MU has great fundamentals but the chart is broken post-earnings. Wait for the selling pressure to exhaust and a volume-confirmed bounce before re-entering.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon is 5.5% below a declining 50-day average of $219.34, RSI at 43.9 — neutral-bearish with no technical improvement. Two developments from yesterday's news: AWS is reportedly developing an AI agent to automate jobs (per The Information, cited by Yahoo Finance), and Amazon acquired Fauna Robotics (the Sprout humanoid robot), per Associated Press Finance. Neither moves the near-term thesis. The core headwind remains the $200B AI capex cycle confirmed in the Q4 2025 earnings call — massive investment that sent shares down 10% on missed Q1 operating income guidance, raising the question of when capex translates to earnings. At 22.2x forward PE with a PEG of 1.92 and 13.6% revenue growth, Amazon trades at its BigTech cluster median — not cheap enough to compensate for the capex overhang. FCF yield at 1.07% is very low; the thesis depends entirely on future earnings delivery. The R:R to nearest resistance at $219.43 is 1.2:1 with invalidation at $196.93 (5% below) — unfavourable. Earnings are 37 days out. Bottom line: Amazon is fairly priced but the near-term earnings picture is clouded by the capex cycle. Not attractive at current levels — wait for a pullback to $196-197 support or a pullback to SMA50 reclaim with volume.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Google fell 3.31% on above-average volume — the volume confirmation of a downside move is a meaningful deterioration signal. GOOG is now 8.6% below a declining 50-day average of $316.53, RSI at 32.4 — approaching oversold territory. The R:R is deeply unfavourable: only 0.8% upside to nearest resistance at $291.53 versus 9.6% downside to the 200-day average of $261.39 (the invalidation level), giving a 0.1:1 R:R ratio. The 200-day SMA at $261.39 is the next structural support level. The Golden Dome defense contract (per Wall Street Journal and Reuters) through the Alphabet-adapted Aalyria Technologies networking spinout is a positive read-through but does not directly benefit GOOG's core revenue. The Openreach/Google Cloud AI partnership for fibre rollout, per Reuters, is incremental. The primary concern: analysts project 31.1% earnings growth, but the stock is selling off on above-average volume, suggesting institutional distribution. At 21.5x forward PE — in line with the BigTech median — there is no valuation discount to compensate. PEG of 2.24 is elevated. Earnings on April 24 (30 days out) are the binary event that could resolve the thesis. Contrarian RSI check: RSI 32.4 is below 38 — GOOG is evaluated for contrarian candidacy in the relevant section. Bottom line: Google is approaching oversold levels, but the chart is broken and getting worse. No entry until the selling pressure stops — $261 (200-day average) is the first meaningful support to watch.

LITE.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 31% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Lumentum surged 9.86% yesterday and is now 44.5% above its 50-day average — a hard block with no override. The entry block is absolute: extreme extension at this level requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration. The NVDA $2B strategic investment (confirmed via NVIDIA investor.nvidia.com press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion on March 9 are the twin structural catalysts, but inclusion buying exhausts within 3-5 sessions per established heuristics, and LITE has been above its 50-day SMA for 202 sessions. The PEG of 0.16 appears anomalous — with a forward PE of 53.5x, the implied earnings growth rate would need to be extraordinarily high to produce a PEG this low; verify before citing. FCF yield is -0.04% (negative — flag prominently). Morgan Stanley raised its price target to $595 from $520 with an Equal Weight rating per Insider Monkey, implying downside from current $802. Barron's notes the explosive gains with 'room to rise' but $595 is still 26% below current price. The sma50_warning applies: do not suggest pullback-to-SMA50 as an entry. The nearest shorter-term supports are $842.09 (resistance above) and $573.73-$548.24 zone below. Bottom line: LITE has extraordinary momentum driven by real catalysts, but at 44% above its moving average, this is not an entry — the risk of a sharp reversal is too high regardless of the thesis strength.

XLE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. XLE is 13.2% above its 50-day average with RSI at 80.5 — deeply overbought and hard-blocked from entry. The Hormuz crisis entering week 3 is the sole driver, and energy stocks rose another 2.19% yesterday. The R:R is poor at 0.4:1 using derived support at the 50-day SMA. If the Iran peace-deal headline reported by Yahoo Finance (citing the New York Times) translates into a genuine ceasefire, XLE could give back its entire Hormuz premium rapidly — the ETF has gained 10.3% in a month purely on geopolitical fear. WTI data has a conflict (discussed in market_pulse) but directionally, a peace deal would push oil sharply lower and XLE would follow. Trailing PE of 23.4x is elevated for an energy ETF. No entry at current levels under any scenario — the upside is capped at $63.88 (5% away) while the downside in a Hormuz resolution scenario could approach the pre-crisis level near the 50-day SMA of $53.76 (11.6% below). The 2.62% dividend yield does not compensate for the technical risk at RSI 80. Bottom line: XLE is at an extreme — either the Hormuz crisis gets worse (diminishing returns as most of the move is priced) or it resolves (sharp unwind). No attractive entry in either scenario at current levels.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 83x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — Palantir's Golden Dome defense software role, confirmed by both Reuters and the Wall Street Journal, represents a material positive development. Per Reuters, Palantir and Anduril are developing software for the Golden Dome missile shield with testing targeted for this summer. The $10B Army IDIQ framework (ceiling amount, not firm revenue) and the $448M Navy ShipOS deal (confirmed via DoD press release per catalysts) provide near-term revenue visibility. Rosenblatt raised its price target to $200, per catalyst notes. PLTR is 2.6% above its 50-day average of $150.79 with RSI at 53.9 and above-average volume — volume confirms the move. The invalidation is at $150.79 (the 50-day SMA, 2.6% below). Structural support at $148.06 (4.3% below) is the more meaningful thesis test. The R:R to the nearest resistance at $187.08 is 8.0:1 — a highly favourable ratio. The primary obstacle to ACCUMULATE is the valuation: 82.9x forward PE (273% premium to the BigTech cluster median of 22.2x) with a PEG of 2.98 and FCF yield of 0.34%. The entry block flags the 83x forward PE as requiring explicit catalyst justification. The Golden Dome contract news IS that catalyst, and with 70% revenue growth and 648% projected earnings growth, the growth rate partially justifies the multiple — but 83x remains aggressive. At entry, the 2026 revenue guide of $7.18-7.20B (+61% YoY per catalysts) is the primary valuation anchor. Earnings are 41 days out. ACCUMULATE gate check: R:R 8.0:1 passes, rr_quality observed, caution_source was base_scorer, VIX < 30. However, the forward PE of 82.9x well exceeds 60x without explicit justification warranting exception. The framework blocks ACCUMULATE when forward PE > 60x without explicit justification — a defense AI platform with 61% YoY revenue growth and Golden Dome contract momentum is a reasonable justification, but this is a judgement call. Given the extreme valuation premium (273% above cluster median) and the binary nature of defense contract wins, I will assign WATCH rather than ACCUMULATE, watching for pullback to the SMA50 level for a cleaner entry. Bottom line: PLTR is the most exciting momentum name on the watchlist right now — defense AI is a real and growing revenue stream. Fairly priced to expensive depending on your growth assumptions. Enter on a pullback to $150-152 with a stop at $148.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Watch for a reclaim of $130.24 resistance with volume — the Iran peace-deal headline could trigger a relief bounce at today's open.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster enters its 15th consecutive cautious session with no reset signal. OCBC (O39_SI) is the sole divergence — reclaiming its 50-day SMA and upgrading to WATCH — while DBS, UOB, SIA, and Singtel remain below their respective averages. The cluster-level unlock remains dual-gated: US10Y must break below 4.20% (currently 4.39%) AND the STI must stabilise above 5,000 — neither condition is met.
singapore.key_development: OCBC's SMA50 reclaim creates a rare intra-cluster divergence: OCBC's NIM resilience is outperforming peers, validating its selective positioning even as the broader Singapore bank thesis remains blocked.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX closed at $341.02 (+1.05%), recovering from Monday's sharp selloff, but the cluster remains bifurcated: ASML upgraded to WATCH on the SK Hynix $7.9B EUV commitment, AMD upgraded to HOLD on Meta deal recovery, while NVDA, INTC, and MU stay on CAUTION. The mid-cap foundry vs. mega-cap duration divergence is narrowing as MU's post-earnings selling pressure drags the thesis.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Mixed — foundry mid-cap resilience vs. mega-cap duration headwinds
semiconductors.key_development: The SK Hynix-ASML deal (reported by GuruFocus) independently validates EUV demand outside Intel, providing ASML with a second major customer anchor that reduces Intel execution dependency.

big_tech.summary: GOOG's 3.31% decline on above-average volume is the cluster's dominant signal — institutional distribution ahead of April 24 earnings. AMZN holds its CAUTION with the capex overhang intact. PLTR breaks away from the cluster weakness, upgrading to WATCH on Golden Dome defense contract news — a structurally different revenue driver from commercial BigTech capex.
big_tech.key_development: PLTR's Golden Dome role, confirmed by Reuters and WSJ, fundamentally differentiates it from the AMZN/GOOG capex sustainability concern — defense AI budgets are less sensitive to rate cycles than commercial hyperscaler spend.

photonics.summary: LITE surged 9.86% and COHR 6.15% — both continuing the post-NVDA investment and S&P 500 inclusion momentum. Both remain hard-blocked from entry (LITE at 44.5% above SMA50, COHR at 17%). COHR upgrades from CAUTION to HOLD as the structural catalyst is now validated; LITE stays CAUTION with the extreme extension block absolute.
photonics.thesis_status: Structurally bullish — blocked from entry on extension
photonics.key_development: Barron's featured both LITE and COHR as S&P 500 leaders with room to rise, but Morgan Stanley's Equal Weight with a $595 target on LITE implies 26% downside from current levels — a notable divergence of view worth tracking.

energy.summary: XLE remains deeply overbought at RSI 80.5 and 13.2% above its 50-day average — entirely Hormuz-driven. The Iran peace-deal headline from Yahoo Finance (citing the New York Times) is the single most important overnight development for this cluster; a confirmed ceasefire would unwind the Hormuz premium sharply.
energy.key_development: US peace offer to Iran, per New York Times reporting cited by Yahoo Finance, represents the first de-escalation signal in 16 days — if confirmed, this is the primary catalyst that would move XLE from CAUTION to avoid territory as the supply premium unwinds.

index.summary: VUAA_L at $126.70 remains 3.7% below a declining 50-day average with RSI 36.7 — deeply oversold but no reversal signal yet. The Iran peace-deal headline could trigger a gap-open relief bounce. 6500 SPY support ($660 area) remains the critical test.
index.key_development: Futures reportedly jumped on the US-Iran peace deal offer per Yahoo Finance — today's open is the first real test of whether the 16-day selloff in global indices can find a floor.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC)
interconnected.reason: NVDA Blackwell Ultra ramp and AMD MI450 production (Meta deal) flow through TSMC's advanced nodes — utilisation rates are a direct read on both theses simultaneously.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM ADR pulls back 8-10% from current levels or if NVDA's next earnings print confirms accelerating Blackwell shipments.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: Custom AI ASIC demand from hyperscalers (Google TPU adjacency, Meta networking) directly benefits Broadcom's custom chip and networking division — a read-through to GOOG and AMZN capex themes.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if AVGO pulls back toward its 50-day SMA — revisit after GOOG earnings on April 24 for hyperscaler capex confirmation.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: The $7.9B ASML EUV commitment (reported by GuruFocus) confirms SK Hynix is aggressively expanding HBM capacity — a direct read on ASML's order book and MU's competitive positioning in AI memory.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit if SK Hynix ADR equivalent or KRW-denominated shares pull back 10%+ from current levels following the ASML capex commitment announcement.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz crisis week 3: Strait shipping traffic near zero (2 vessels vs 138 baseline); WTI in $87-92 range with data conflicts; US reportedly offered Iran a peace deal per NYT
Fed hawkish rate pause: rates at 3.50%-3.75%, US10Y at 4.39%, sticky inflation constraining rate-cut probability and compressing tech multiples
US-China semiconductor supply chain reshaping: 100% tariffs, software export bans, China retaliating with 34% tariffs and import controls on NVIDIA processors
S&P 500 technical fragility: SPY 1.2% below 100-day average, VIX at 26.95 reflecting sustained fear premium
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz disruption has pushed XLE to RSI 80.5 — overbought and vulnerable to a sharp reversal on any de-escalation signal. GOOG's 3.31% decline on above-average volume reflects institutional hedging ahead of April 24 earnings as the Fed's higher-for-longer stance compresses growth multiples; PLTR diverges positively as defense AI budgets are structurally insulated from rate-cycle sensitivity.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Per Yahoo Finance (citing the New York Times), the US offered Iran a peace deal — futures jumped on the news. If confirmed, this is the first material de-escalation in 16 days and would represent a significant shift in the Hormuz risk premium. Additionally, Palantir and Anduril were confirmed by Reuters and the Wall Street Journal as the lead software developers for the Golden Dome missile shield, targeting testing this summer.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash reserves; selective watch-list entries only in names with specific catalysts (PLTR on pullback to $150-152, OCBC on volume-confirmed SMA50 hold) — avoid energy and broad index exposure until the Iran peace-deal outcome clarifies.

events_this_week.event: Iran peace deal clarity — US-Iran negotiations outcome (per NYT/Yahoo Finance)
GOOG distribution signal — above-average volume decline warrants monitoring for follow-through at open

Non-Farm Payrolls (March) — 08:30 ET. A strong print reinforces the Fed's rate-pause thesis (bearish for tech multiples); a weak print could revive rate-cut expectations and provide relief to NVDA, AMZN, GOOG.
ASML Q1 2026 earnings — 21 days out. Could resolve the WATCH signal: strong bookings confirm the SK Hynix/Intel EUV ramp thesis; a miss on order intake would revert to CAUTION.
Intel (INTC) Q1 2026 earnings — binary event for the 18A foundry thesis. Negative FCF at current levels requires evidence of inflection.
Google (GOOG) Q1 2026 earnings — key test for cloud growth sustainability and hyperscaler capex guidance amid institutional distribution signal.
AMD MI450 / Meta GPU deal first 1GW shipment milestone (H2 2026) — confirmed via AMD IR press release; first hardware validation of the Meta partnership that underpins the WATCH-to-BUY upgrade path.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The Fed's rate pause at 3.50%-3.75% with US10Y at 4.39% is the single most pervasive risk — it compresses multiples across 9 of 11 CAUTION names simultaneously, with no resolution until inflation resets below the Fed's threshold.

watch_for_entry.note: Golden Dome defense role confirmed by Reuters/WSJ. Enter on pullback to $150-152 (50-day SMA), stop below $148. Half position given extreme 83x forward PE valuation.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter $150-152 on SMA50 reclaim, stop below $148.06

watch_for_entry.note: Only Singapore bank above its 50-day SMA with NIM resilience. Tight stop at SGD 21.04 — confirm with volume before entering. Starter position.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter SGD 21.05-21.20 on volume confirmation, stop below SGD 21.04

watch_for_entry.note: SK Hynix $7.9B EUV deal validates order book. Tight stop at $1,392 — wait for volume confirmation. Earnings April 15 argues for patience.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter $1,393-1,400, stop below $1,392.48. Size small ahead of April 15 earnings.

on_deck.note: Best fundamental case on the watchlist — 0.71 PEG at 15.8x forward PE with 73% revenue growth. Technically blocked below declining 50-day average.
on_deck.rationale: Revenue growth of 73.2% massively exceeds the 15.8x forward PE; PEG 0.71 makes this the cheapest quality name on the board. Blackwell Ultra ramp provides multi-quarter catalyst.
on_deck.trigger: 50-day average flattens and price reclaims $184.47 resistance with above-average volume, OR VIX drops below 22 and NVDA holds $169.81 support with conviction.

on_deck.note: Meta GPU deal confirmed via AMD IR press release — 6GW deployment validates hyperscale ambitions. PEG 0.6 at 19.1x forward PE with 34% revenue growth.
on_deck.rationale: 217% consensus earnings growth with a confirmed multi-year hyperscaler supply deal makes AMD the clearest catalyst story in the semis cluster after NVDA.
on_deck.trigger: AMD reclaims 50-day SMA at $214.45 with above-average volume on a day SPY holds flat or rises.
